title: Robust Peppery Northern Thai Lamb Khanom Thai
description: A peppery and tender lamb dish from Northern Thailand, made with a mortar and pestle and cooked on a tava griddle, perfect for a satvik friendly meal.
instructions: Pound the black peppercorns, coriander seeds, and cumin seeds in a mortar and pestle until coarsely ground.
Add the sliced galangal and bruised lemon grass to the mortar and pestle and pound until well combined.
Heat the coconut oil in a tava griddle over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces to the tava griddle and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the pounded spice mixture, fish sauce, and salt to the tava griddle and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until the lamb is cooked through.
Stir in the fresh kaffir lime leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Serve the lamb hot, garnished with additional kaffir lime leaves if desired.
